provenance.source_corpus: Large Woody Debris Structures and Their Influence on Atlantic Salmon Spawning in a Stream in Nova Scotia, Canada

source_spans.text: Spawning rates (redd counts) in the whole brook increased exponentially for the first 4 years after restoration work began, from 43 in 1992 to 592 in 1996.
Artificial structures mimicking naturally fallen wood are effective in the restoration of spawning habitat.
Spawning rates (redd counts) in the whole brook increased exponentially for the first 4 years after restoration work began, from 43 in 1992 to 592 in 1996.
Artificial structures mimicking naturally fallen wood are effective in the restoration of spawning habitat.
suggesting that restoration near the mouth of the brook removed an impediment to upstream migration.
Large woody debris, whether natural or artificial, appears to be an important source of spawning habitat for Atlantic salmon.
